item.title: She Made Her Lover Do It!
item.description:
Jamie Faith appeared to have been the victim of a random ambush when he was shot seven times while walking his dog outside his Dallas home in 2020. His wife, Jennifer Faith, tearfully told police and the media she had watched a stranger murder her husband and attack her. But investigators uncovered a far darker truth. Jennifer had secretly manipulated her boyfriend, Darrin Lopez, into carrying out the killing by creating fake emails that falsely claimed Jamie was physically and sexually abusing her. She even used stolen photos of injuries to support the lie. After the murder, she collected donations, pursued a $629,000 life insurance payout, and helped Lopez cover his tracks. In 2022, Jennifer pleaded guilty to murder-for-hire and was sentenced to life in federal prison, while a judge described her actions as “pure evil.”
Do you think Jennifer’s elaborate deception was motivated mainly by love, greed, or a desire for control—and could anyone around her have spotted the warning signs before it was too late?

media:content.media:title: She Made Her Lover Do It!
media:content.media:description:
Jamie Faith appeared to have been the victim of a random ambush when he was shot seven times while walking his dog outside his Dallas home in 2020. His wife, Jennifer Faith, tearfully told police and the media she had watched a stranger murder her husband and attack her. But investigators uncovered a far darker truth. Jennifer had secretly manipulated her boyfriend, Darrin Lopez, into carrying out the killing by creating fake emails that falsely claimed Jamie was physically and sexually abusing her. She even used stolen photos of injuries to support the lie. After the murder, she collected donations, pursued a $629,000 life insurance payout, and helped Lopez cover his tracks. In 2022, Jennifer pleaded guilty to murder-for-hire and was sentenced to life in federal prison, while a judge described her actions as “pure evil.”
Do you think Jennifer’s elaborate deception was motivated mainly by love, greed, or a desire for control—and could anyone around her have spotted the warning signs before it was too late?

item.title: Aaron Davis Couldn't Even Fight for His Life
item.description: Aaron Davis’s death has drawn attention to the circumstances surrounding the case and the need for accountability and careful investigation. This video focuses on verified reporting and the

media:content.media:title: Aaron Davis Couldn't Even Fight for His Life
media:content.media:description: Aaron Davis’s death has drawn attention to the circumstances surrounding the case and the need for accountability and careful investigation. This video focuses on verified reporting and the

item.title: Footage Shows Teen Left in the Hot Car for 6 Hours!
item.description:
Cristina Pangalangan was a 13-year-old girl with cerebral palsy who relied completely on others for care. On a scorching day in South Carolina in August 2019, she was left strapped inside the back seat of a car for nearly six hours while her mother, Rita Pangalangan, and her boyfriend, Larry King Jr., spent the day nearby using methamphetamine, arguing, and having sex. Unable to speak, walk, or free herself, Cristina endured temperatures that climbed to an estimated 135°F (57°C). By the time anyone checked on her, she had died from hyperthermia. In 2023, both adults were convicted of murder and child abuse, with prosecutors describing the case as a heartbreaking example of neglect that cost a helpless child her life. Cristina was remembered as a loving girl who enjoyed cartoons and brought joy to those around her.
What do you think should happen when caregivers ignore a vulnerable child’s basic needs—was the prison sentence enough, or should there have been even greater accountability?

media:content.media:title: Footage Shows Teen Left in the Hot Car for 6 Hours!
media:content.media:description:
Cristina Pangalangan was a 13-year-old girl with cerebral palsy who relied completely on others for care. On a scorching day in South Carolina in August 2019, she was left strapped inside the back seat of a car for nearly six hours while her mother, Rita Pangalangan, and her boyfriend, Larry King Jr., spent the day nearby using methamphetamine, arguing, and having sex. Unable to speak, walk, or free herself, Cristina endured temperatures that climbed to an estimated 135°F (57°C). By the time anyone checked on her, she had died from hyperthermia. In 2023, both adults were convicted of murder and child abuse, with prosecutors describing the case as a heartbreaking example of neglect that cost a helpless child her life. Cristina was remembered as a loving girl who enjoyed cartoons and brought joy to those around her.
What do you think should happen when caregivers ignore a vulnerable child’s basic needs—was the prison sentence enough, or should there have been even greater accountability?
